subject: Sorry we've missed you!
body:
Hi, Cade!

If youve received this response, it means the email you sent us safely made it to our inbox. We push ourselves to give our all Monday-Friday and use weekends to reenergize, spend time with loved ones and catch up on laundry. Come Monday morning, well be back to you as you soon as we open our laptops.

If you emailed something that cant wait, such as the ads are down on your site, send your email to emergency@monumetric.com < mailto:emergency@monumetric.com > and well be right on it.

We thank you for your partnership and hope you have a great weekend as well!

Monumetric Publisher Success Team

[Image alt text: Monumetric Logo]

subject: Introduction to your Monumetric Publisher Success Team
body:
Hi Cade- Yes, the changes that were made will be live when you activate the plugin. :) I am happy to let you know that I have activated your Monumetric dashboard for pennycentral.com and your ads are now live! www.onboarding.monumetric.com You'll notice a purple Monumetric Logo on the bottom right-hand side of your dashboard. That magic button will lead you to resources that can instantly answer almost any question you may have. In case you can't find your answers there, I want to introduce you to your Publisher Success Team. They will be your go-to for any questions or issues with anything related to your ads. You will receive an email from them shortly with additional information on what to expect and how to contact them. They are seriously fantastic, so you'll love working with them! success@monumetric.com Ramp-Up: As a reminder, there is a ramp-up period of typically 30 days when you join our network. During this time, advertisers are still acclimating to your site and determining their spend. We appreciate your patience during this process. Ad Layout Changes: We are happy to work with you to make any changes to your ads now or in the future as needed. Please just let us know beforehand so that we can work together to make sure any changes made are still performing and displaying properly. What works for one site, won't always work for another and we don't succeed unless you succeed. Terms and Conditions: Lastly, I invite you to familiarize yourself with our terms and conditions, specifically as it relates to a 30-day notice in section 11 should you ever choose to leave us, we just hope that never happens. It's been an absolute pleasure working with you. Welcome to Monumetric! Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com] Sent: 2/27/2026, 12:45 PM To: sales@monumetric.com Subject: Re: Almost there! Hi Samantha, Thanks again for the quick turnaround and for coordinating with your team. I want to restart ads today for a controlled weekend test. Monumetric is still disabled on my side because the initial rollout was too aggressive for our UX, and I should have been more hands-on up front. Before I flip it back on, can you confirm the live configuration is: Mobile: no sticky header or anchor placements (nothing that can overlap or interfere with nav). Interstitial/vignette: off. Video/autoplay: off. /report-find excluded for all variants (including query parameters). Refresh no faster than once per 60 seconds. Weekend placement: in-content and footer only on /, /penny-list, and /guide (no header/nav placements, and avoid back-to-back placements). If anything above is still in progress or propagating, please let me know what is pending and the ETA. Once confirmed, Ill re-enable and send a Monday UX/performance update so we can tune together next week. Also yes, please introduce me to the success team and send dashboard access. Thanks, Cade From: noreply@salesforce.com on behalf of Monetization Specialists Monumetric Sent: Friday, February 27, 2026 7:30 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Re: Almost there! Hi Cade, Here are the changes that our team has made as requested: 1. Updated the mobile header in-screen so it wont cover the header navigation. 2. Interstitial has been disabled. 3. Excluded our ads from /report-find URL. 4. Keep ads /penny-list, and /guide. 5. Video ad has been disabled. Please check your site and let me know if you are ready to introduce you to the rest of the success team and give you access to the dashboard. :) Thanks, Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Monetization Specialists Monumetric [sales@monumetric.com] Sent: 2/26/2026, 12:56 PM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Re: Almost there! Hi Cade, I have removed the interstitial ad and have asked our team to make the other changes ASAP. I will be in touch as soon as I hear back from them. Thanks, Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com] Sent: 2/26/2026, 12:38 PM To: sales@monumetric.com Subject: Re: Almost there! Hi Samantha, Thank you for the implementation work. I need immediate configuration changes to protect user experience and long-term site health. Since the evening of February 25, 2026, our average engagement per session dropped suddenly from roughly 1:40 down to about 0:22. The mobile top sticky banner is blocking/interfering with navigation (header/hamburger), and the site is unusable for many mobile users. Please make these updates as soon as possible: 1) Remove/disable the mobile top sticky header banner for now, and prioritize a permanent fix ASAP so navigation is always clickable. 2) Disable interstitial/vignette/takeover formats sitewide. 3) Hard-exclude ads from https://www.pennycentral.com/report-find and all /report-find URL variants (including query parameters). 4) Keep remaining ads non-obstructive on /, /penny-list, and /guide (no overlap with header/nav controls). 5) Pause video ad rollout for now. Technical references from our audits include: - mmt-sticky-header-div - google_ads_iframe_/20842576,23336802101/MJM03M/MJM03M-DMH.A_* - #google_vignette - google_ads_iframe_/20842576,23336802101/MJM03M/MJM03M-DMW.A_0 Please confirm by reply: - exactly what was disabled/changed - the /report-find exclusion rule applied - expected propagation time - when the mobile nav-blocking issue is fully resolved Thanks, Cade Allen From: noreply@salesforce.com on behalf of Monetization Specialists Monumetric Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 10:31 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Almost there! Hello Cade, We have done our final inspection of the ad implementation on your site, and everything looks good on our end. We need your help to implement the video and in-content ads. Here are the tags: Video ad -
In-content Repeatable -
Before I introduce you to our Publisher Success Team (which I'm also a part of), I would like to ask you to take a look at your site (oftentimes, you'll need to do this in an incognito window) to make sure you are happy with the ad placements. Please note that once we introduce you to our Publisher Success Team, and open up access to your dashboard, there will be a 30-day ramp-up period until advertisers get familiar with bidding on your site. I look forward to hearing back from you! Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Monetization Specialists Monumetric [sales@monumetric.com] Sent: 2/26/2026, 8:14 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Re: We haven't forgotten about you! Hi Cade, Thank you for letting me know. I have passed this to our team and have asked if there are any remaining blockers. I will try to get you an answer by the end of the day. Please know that our team is working on several sites. I think your ads will live by Monday at the latest. Thanks, Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com] Sent: 2/25/2026, 1:14 PM To: sales@monumetric.com Subject: Re: We haven't forgotten about you! Hi Samantha, CSP has been updated and is live on production. Both securepubads.g.doubleclick.net and cdn.confiant-integrations.net are now allowed. Please have your team re-test immediately. I need confirmation today on: Whether there are any remaining blockers on my end The exact date ads will go live I have completed all requested implementation steps and need this moved to launch. Thanks, Cade From: noreply@salesforce.com on behalf of Monetization Specialists Monumetric Sent: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 10:13 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: RE: We haven't forgotten about you! Hi Cade, Thanks for reaching out. Yes, I can confirm that your site is being worked on. Here is an update from our implementation team: The publishers CSP is blocking a script from doubleclick.net. To resolve it, add https://securepubads.g.doubleclick.net to the script-src directive in CSP, either in HTML tag or server's HTTP headers. This also includes the script from https://cdn.confiant-integrations.net. In the HTML meta tag update it as follows: Please let me know when you have completed this so we can move forward. Thanks, Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: [no-reply@monumetric.com] Sent: 2/23/2026, 9:00 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: We haven't forgotten about you! This is an update to let you know that our team is currently still working on your request/concern. We want to make sure we do a thorough job of supporting you! We truly appreciate your patience and will be following up soon with more details. Thanks again! The Monumetric Team

subject:
RE: URGENT: Mobile Site Functionality Compromised | Final
 Escalation - pennycentral.com
body:
Hi Cade,

I'm sorry to hear you are having these issues with your ads. Here is the information from our implementation team:

We did see the issue with the header inscreen and this happened because the Main Content container sits underneath the navigation bar, and there is a hidden part of the navigation that does show up on some pages, which the header inscreen did cover up. So the header in-screen does not really work for your site.

Per your request:

All mobile header, top sticky, anchor, and floating placements are fully disabled.

I removed the header inscreen on Mobile.

I removed the Footer inscreen on Mobile.


No ad unit may overlap, cover, follow, or interfere with the site header or navigation.

By removing the header inscreen this should fix the issue they were having with the ad covering their navigation.


Interstitial and vignette formats remain off.

This was already disabled


Video and autoplay remain off.

Video was already disabled


The `/report-find` route remains excluded for all variants.

This was done in the initial imp


The actual live refresh interval currently applied.

We have turned off Allow Oveerrides, so refresh will be the same for every ad


Which placements are currently live by route and position, including whether any in-content units are active. "

In-content:

Devices: Desktop, Tablet & Mobile

Container = .py-8

Inserting BEFORE every 3rd <h2>


Top Sidebar

Not being inserted.


Header Inscreen

Devices: Desktop & Tablet

Inserting AFTER nav.sticky


Left Pillar

Devices: Desktop

Inserting AFTER body


Middle Sidebar

Not being inserted.


Footer Inscreen

Devices: Desktop & Tablet

Inserting AFTER body


Sticky Sidebar

Not being inserted.


Interstitial

Not being inserted


Video

Not being inserted.





Please let us know if you have additional questions or requests.

Thanks,


Samantha Melaney
Publisher Success Associate
42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025
Schedule a Call Here









--------------- Original Message ---------------
From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com]
Sent: 3/3/2026, 5:17 PM
To: success@monumetric.com
Subject: URGENT: Mobile Site Functionality Compromised | Final Escalation - pennycentral.com

Hi Amy,
I am formally escalating a critical technical failure regarding the Monumetric implementation on pennycentral.com. Ads remain disabled because the current implementation repeatedly breaks my mobile navigation and UI.
Despite multiple "fix" confirmations from your team, the issues persist. This is a custom Next.js environment, and it appears the current ad injection logic is conflicting with the React lifecycle or CSS layout/z-index.
To continue this partnership, I require the following technical clarifications by EOD:

Single Point of Contact: Provide the name and role of the technical lead responsible for this ticket.
Current Configuration: Confirm that sticky headers, top banners, and interstitials are disabled at the account level.
Reproduction Environment: Confirm the specific physical mobile device and browser used to verify the "fix" (emulators are insufficient).
Conflict Resolution: What specific adjustments are being made to the script to prevent interference with the site’s header and navigation?
In-Content Logic: Why are the provided in-content scripts failing to hook into the DOM, and what is the remediation plan?
Testing Protocol: Provide an ETA for a stable environment. I prefer a limited/staging test before any further production rollouts.

My goal is a stable, mutually beneficial partnership. However, the repeated "fixed" claims followed by immediate production breakage have exhausted my confidence. If we cannot establish a concrete path to resolution today, I will move to terminate the relationship and void any onboarding commitments due to implementation non-performance.
I look forward to your immediate response.
Best regards,
Cade


From: noreply@salesforce.com <noreply@salesforce.com> on behalf of Publisher Success Monumetric <success@monumetric.com>
Sent: Monday, March 2, 2026 12:06 PM
To: cadegallen@gmail.com <cadegallen@gmail.com>
Subject: RE: Fwd: success@monumetric.com

Hi Cade,

Thank you for your email! I'm sorry that these issues are still showing up. I've sent your message over to our Implementation team and we will get back to you as soon as we have an update.



Amy Whitney
Publisher Success Associate
42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025
Schedule a Call Here









--------------- Original Message ---------------
From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com]
Sent: 2/27/2026, 5:33 PM
To: success@monumetric.com
Subject: Fwd: success@monumetric.com

Placed "success@monumetric.com" in the subject line instead of "cc".

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Cade allen  <cadegallen@gmail.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 27, 2026 at 7:29 PM
Subject:  success@monumetric.com
To: <emergency@monumetric.com>


Hi Monumetric Team,

I am escalating this as an emergency because the current live ad behavior is making the mobile site unusable.

I re-enabled Monumetric today to validate the changes I was told were live, and I observed the same failure mode as before.

On mobile, the following issues are occurring:

• The top/header ad unit is interfering with the site header and navigation.
• The unit appears clipped on initial load.
• It follows or sticks while scrolling.
• It flickers, blanks out, and appears to reload or refresh repeatedly.
• The header/navigation cannot be used reliably in this state.

This is not a minor UX issue or a tuning issue; it is a functional blocking issue.

The initial rollout coincided with a sharp drop in engagement metrics and forced me to disable Monumetric entirely. I only re-enabled after being told this issue had been fixed, but the live behavior shows the same failure is still present.

I have disabled Monumetric again on my side.

For clarity, PennyCentral is a custom Next.js site, not a WordPress site. There is no WordPress plugin layer on our end. The live ad behavior is coming from the Monumetric configuration and scripts running on our site.

I have attached a short mobile screen recording showing the current live behavior.

Before I consider turning Monumetric back on, I need confirmation from your implementation team that the following are complete and live in production:

• All mobile header, top sticky, anchor, and floating placements are fully disabled.
• No ad unit may overlap, cover, follow, or interfere with the site header or navigation.
• Interstitial and vignette formats remain off.
• Video and autoplay remain off.
• The `/report-find` route remains excluded for all variants.
• The actual live refresh interval currently applied.
• Which placements are currently live by route and position, including whether any in-content units are active.

Please reply with:

• What has actually been changed in the live configuration.
• What is still pending.
• The expected propagation time.
• Confirmation once your team has verified in production that the mobile header/navigation issue is fully resolved.

I understand some of this may need to come directly from implementation. That is fine. What I need now is accurate technical confirmation and a real fix before any re-enable.

This must be fully resolved before I will consider turning Monumetric back on.

Thanks,

Cade

ScreenRecording_02-27-2026 18-51-20_1.MP4

subject: Action Needed: Finalize your Monumetric Implementation
body:
Hi Cade,

Great news! We’re ready to move your site into implementation! To get started, we’ll need a few things from you. Please log in to onboarding.monumetric.com and complete the following steps:

	Select Your Implementation Preference (Monumetric, Self Implementation, or Hybrid).
	 
	If you select Monumetric, please provide your site credentials by creating or adding an admin account using tech@monumetric.com as the email/username.
	
	Also, please add any notes our implementation team should keep in mind when installing your ad code!
	 
	
	
	Select your site’s platform (e.g., WordPress, etc.).
	 

Please note: Your site cannot move forward until all fields are completed.

Once everything is submitted, your site will automatically enter our implementation team’s queue. Once they reach your spot in line, our engineers will then begin setting up and running thorough quality checks to ensure your ads perform smoothly. 

This process usually takes a few days, but can sometimes take longer depending on the site’s specific needs and our work queue. I will update you once implementation is complete or if our team has any questions.

If you need help at any step, don’t hesitate to reach out; I’m here to help.

Excited to get your site up and running!

subject: Welcome to Monumetric!
body:
Congratulations, you have now created your Monumetric Publisher Console account. You may now log in using the username below, and the password you created during signup.

Username:cadegallen@gmail.com
URL:https://console.monumetric.com

Forgot your password? No worries, click here to reset it.

Over the next few weeks, you will be able to login at console.monumetric.com to check on your website's approval status and to send important information to our team to get your site approved by our advertisers. After your site is live with Monumetric ad technology, your Publisher Console will gain new functionality, displaying your advertising metrics as well as important account settings.
Please make sure to complete all of the required steps outlined in your dashboard so that our team can submit your site for approval by our advertising partners. Our team will be in touch with you as soon as possible regarding the next steps.
Feel free to reach out to sales@monumetric.com if you encounter any questions along the way.
We look forward to working with you!
Regards,
Monumetric Team
This email is intended for cadegallen@gmail.com. If you feel you received this email in error please contact your account manager or support@monumetric.com.

In the event of an emergency (ex. ads are not serving), email emergency@monumetric.com and CC the Success Team.

Ad Performance

The Ads Dashboard will update data for the previous day each morning by 10 A.M. MST.
Bookmark this link to access the Monumetric Console.
Learn more about the Ad Performance Dashboard by clicking the "?" icon in the top right corner of the bar graph for a walkthrough.

Watch for additional information coming soon. We are excited about our partnership and look forward to a promising future together!






Amy Whitney
Publisher Success Associate
42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025
Schedule a Call Here

subject: Sorry we've missed you!
body:
Hi, Cade!

If youve received this response, it means the email you sent us safely made it to our inbox. We push ourselves to give our all Monday-Friday and use weekends to reenergize, spend time with loved ones and catch up on laundry. Come Monday morning, well be back to you as you soon as we open our laptops.

If you emailed something that cant wait, such as the ads are down on your site, send your email to emergency@monumetric.com < mailto:emergency@monumetric.com > and well be right on it.

We thank you for your partnership and hope you have a great weekend as well!

Monumetric Publisher Success Team

[Image alt text: Monumetric Logo]

Hi Cade,

Thanks for reaching out. Yes, I can confirm that your site is being worked on. Here is an update from our implementation team:

The publisher’s CSP is blocking a script from  doubleclick.net. To resolve it, add  https://securepubads.g.doubleclick.net to the  script-src directive in CSP, either in HTML  <meta> tag or server's HTTP headers. This also includes the script from  https://cdn.confiant-integrations.net.

In the HTML meta tag update it as follows:

<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' https://www.googletagmanager.com  https://www.google-analytics.com https://va.vercel-scripts.com  https://monu.delivery https://*.monu.delivery  https://faves.grow.me https://*.grow.me https://cdn.id5-sync.com https://*.id5-sync.com https://ep2.adtrafficquality.google https://cdn.confiant-integrations.net;">

subject: Re: Almost there!
body:
Hi Samantha, Thanks again for the quick turnaround and for coordinating with your team. I want to restart ads today for a controlled weekend test. Monumetric is still disabled on my side because the initial rollout was too aggressive for our UX, and I should have been more hands-on up front. Before I flip it back on, can you confirm the live configuration is: 1. Mobile: no sticky header or anchor placements (nothing that can overlap or interfere with nav). 2. Interstitial/vignette: off. 3. Video/autoplay: off. 4. /report-find excluded for all variants (including query parameters). 5. Refresh no faster than once per 60 seconds. 6. Weekend placement: in-content and footer only on /, /penny-list, and /guide (no header/nav placements, and avoid back-to-back placements). If anything above is still in progress or propagating, please let me know what is pending and the ETA. Once confirmed, Ill re-enable and send a Monday UX/performance update so we can tune together next week. Also yes, please introduce me to the success team and send dashboard access. Thanks, Cade ________________________________ From: noreply@salesforce.com on behalf of Monetization Specialists Monumetric Sent: Friday, February 27, 2026 7:30 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Re: Almost there! Hi Cade, Here are the changes that our team has made as requested: 1. Updated the mobile header in-screen so it wont cover the header navigation. 2. Interstitial has been disabled. 3. Excluded our ads from /report-find URL. 4. Keep ads /penny-list, and /guide. 5. Video ad has been disabled. Please check your site and let me know if you are ready to introduce you to the rest of the success team and give you access to the dashboard. :) Thanks, [https://s.djg.sh/bU0oEL] Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here [Monumetric Logo] [Google Preferred Partner] [linked in] [instagram] [Facebok] --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Monetization Specialists Monumetric [sales@monumetric.com] Sent: 2/26/2026, 12:56 PM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Re: Almost there! Hi Samantha, Thank you for the implementation work. I need immediate configuration changes to protect user experience and long-term site health. Since the evening of February 25, 2026, our average engagement per session dropped suddenly from roughly 1:40 down to about 0:22. The mobile top sticky banner is blocking/interfering with navigation (header/hamburger), and the site is unusable for many mobile users. Please make these updates as soon as possible: 1) Remove/disable the mobile top sticky header banner for now, and prioritize a permanent fix ASAP so navigation is always clickable. 2) Disable interstitial/vignette/takeover formats sitewide. 3) Hard-exclude ads from https://www.pennycentral.com/report-find and all /report-find URL variants (including query parameters). 4) Keep remaining ads non-obstructive on /, /penny-list, and /guide (no overlap with header/nav controls). 5) Pause video ad rollout for now. Technical references from our audits include: - mmt-sticky-header-div - google_ads_iframe_/20842576,23336802101/MJM03M/MJM03M-DMH.A_* - #google_vignette - google_ads_iframe_/20842576,23336802101/MJM03M/MJM03M-DMW.A_0 Please confirm by reply: - exactly what was disabled/changed - the /report-find exclusion rule applied - expected propagation time - when the mobile nav-blocking issue is fully resolved Thanks, Cade Allen ________________________________ From: noreply@salesforce.com on behalf of Monetization Specialists Monumetric Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 10:31 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Almost there! Hello Cade, We have done our final inspection of the ad implementation on your site, and everything looks good on our end. We need your help to implement the video and in-content ads. Here are the tags: Video ad -
In-content Repeatable -
Before I introduce you to our Publisher Success Team (which I'm also a part of), I would like to ask you to take a look at your site (oftentimes, you'll need to do this in an incognito window) to make sure you are happy with the ad placements. Please note that once we introduce you to our Publisher Success Team, and open up access to your dashboard, there will be a 30-day ramp-up period until advertisers get familiar with bidding on your site. I look forward to hearing back from you! subject: Re: Almost there!

Hi Cade, I have removed the interstitial ad and have asked our team to make the other changes ASAP. I will be in touch as soon as I hear back from them. Thanks, Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com] Sent: 2/26/2026, 12:38 PM To: sales@monumetric.com Subject: Re: Almost there! Hi Samantha, Thank you for the implementation work. I need immediate configuration changes to protect user experience and long-term site health. Since the evening of February 25, 2026, our average engagement per session dropped suddenly from roughly 1:40 down to about 0:22. The mobile top sticky banner is blocking/interfering with navigation (header/hamburger), and the site is unusable for many mobile users. Please make these updates as soon as possible: 1) Remove/disable the mobile top sticky header banner for now, and prioritize a permanent fix ASAP so navigation is always clickable. 2) Disable interstitial/vignette/takeover formats sitewide. 3) Hard-exclude ads from https://www.pennycentral.com/report-find and all /report-find URL variants (including query parameters). 4) Keep remaining ads non-obstructive on /, /penny-list, and /guide (no overlap with header/nav controls). 5) Pause video ad rollout for now. Technical references from our audits include: - mmt-sticky-header-div - google_ads_iframe_/20842576,23336802101/MJM03M/MJM03M-DMH.A_* - #google_vignette - google_ads_iframe_/20842576,23336802101/MJM03M/MJM03M-DMW.A_0 Please confirm by reply: - exactly what was disabled/changed - the /report-find exclusion rule applied - expected propagation time - when the mobile nav-blocking issue is fully resolved Thanks, Cade Allen From: noreply@salesforce.com on behalf of Monetization Specialists Monumetric Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 10:31 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Almost there! Hello Cade, We have done our final inspection of the ad implementation on your site, and everything looks good on our end. We need your help to implement the video and in-content ads. Here are the tags: Video ad -
In-content Repeatable -
Before I introduce you to our Publisher Success Team (which I'm also a part of), I would like to ask you to take a look at your site (oftentimes, you'll need to do this in an incognito window) to make sure you are happy with the ad placements. Please note that once we introduce you to our Publisher Success Team, and open up access to your dashboard, there will be a 30-day ramp-up period until advertisers get familiar with bidding on your site. I look forward to hearing back from you! Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Monetization Specialists Monumetric [sales@monumetric.com] Sent: 2/26/2026, 8:14 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: Re: We haven't forgotten about you! Hi Cade, Thank you for letting me know. I have passed this to our team and have asked if there are any remaining blockers. I will try to get you an answer by the end of the day. Please know that our team is working on several sites. I think your ads will live by Monday at the latest. Thanks, Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com] Sent: 2/25/2026, 1:14 PM To: sales@monumetric.com Subject: Re: We haven't forgotten about you! Hi Samantha, CSP has been updated and is live on production. Both securepubads.g.doubleclick.net and cdn.confiant-integrations.net are now allowed. Please have your team re-test immediately. I need confirmation today on: Whether there are any remaining blockers on my end The exact date ads will go live I have completed all requested implementation steps and need this moved to launch. Thanks, Cade From: noreply@salesforce.com on behalf of Monetization Specialists Monumetric Sent: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 10:13 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: RE: We haven't forgotten about you! Hi Cade, Thanks for reaching out. Yes, I can confirm that your site is being worked on. Here is an update from our implementation team: The publisher’s CSP is blocking a script from doubleclick.net. To resolve it, add https://securepubads.g.doubleclick.net to the script-src directive in CSP, either in HTML tag or server's HTTP headers. This also includes the script from https://cdn.confiant-integrations.net. In the HTML meta tag update it as follows: Please let me know when you have completed this so we can move forward. Thanks, Samantha Melaney Publisher Success Associate 42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025 Schedule a Call Here --------------- Original Message --------------- From: [no-reply@monumetric.com] Sent: 2/23/2026, 9:00 AM To: cadegallen@gmail.com Subject: We haven't forgotten about you! This is an update to let you know that our team is currently still working on your request/concern. We want to make sure we do a thorough job of supporting you! We truly appreciate your patience and will be following up soon with more details. Thanks again! The Monumetric Team

subject: RE: Fwd: success@monumetric.com
body:
Hi Cade,

Thank you for your email! I'm sorry that these issues are still showing up. I've sent your message over to our Implementation team and we will get back to you as soon as we have an update.



Amy Whitney
Publisher Success Associate
42 N 650 W Farmington, UT 84025
Schedule a Call Here









--------------- Original Message ---------------
From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com]
Sent: 2/27/2026, 5:33 PM
To: success@monumetric.com
Subject: Fwd: success@monumetric.com

Placed "success@monumetric.com" in the subject line instead of "cc".

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Cade allen <cadegallen@gmail.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 27, 2026 at 7:29 PM
Subject: success@monumetric.com
To: <emergency@monumetric.com>


Hi Monumetric Team,

I am escalating this as an emergency because the current live ad behavior is making the mobile site unusable.

I re-enabled Monumetric today to validate the changes I was told were live, and I observed the same failure mode as before.

On mobile, the following issues are occurring:

• The top/header ad unit is interfering with the site header and navigation.
• The unit appears clipped on initial load.
• It follows or sticks while scrolling.
• It flickers, blanks out, and appears to reload or refresh repeatedly.
• The header/navigation cannot be used reliably in this state.

This is not a minor UX issue or a tuning issue; it is a functional blocking issue.

The initial rollout coincided with a sharp drop in engagement metrics and forced me to disable Monumetric entirely. I only re-enabled after being told this issue had been fixed, but the live behavior shows the same failure is still present.

I have disabled Monumetric again on my side.

For clarity, PennyCentral is a custom Next.js site, not a WordPress site. There is no WordPress plugin layer on our end. The live ad behavior is coming from the Monumetric configuration and scripts running on our site.

I have attached a short mobile screen recording showing the current live behavior.

Before I consider turning Monumetric back on, I need confirmation from your implementation team that the following are complete and live in production:

• All mobile header, top sticky, anchor, and floating placements are fully disabled.
• No ad unit may overlap, cover, follow, or interfere with the site header or navigation.
• Interstitial and vignette formats remain off.
• Video and autoplay remain off.
• The `/report-find` route remains excluded for all variants.
• The actual live refresh interval currently applied.
• Which placements are currently live by route and position, including whether any in-content units are active.

Please reply with:

• What has actually been changed in the live configuration.
• What is still pending.
• The expected propagation time.
• Confirmation once your team has verified in production that the mobile header/navigation issue is fully resolved.

I understand some of this may need to come directly from implementation. That is fine. What I need now is accurate technical confirmation and a real fix before any re-enable.

This must be fully resolved before I will consider turning Monumetric back on.

Thanks,

Cade

ScreenRecording_02-27-2026 18-51-20_1.MP4

body:
Hi Cade,

Thanks for starting your application to join Monumetric! We noticed your setup in our onboarding dashboard isn’t fully complete yet. Once you finish adding your site’s business details at onboarding.monumetric.com, we’ll be able to move forward with your site’s onboarding process. 

If you have any questions or run into any snags, don’t hesitate to reach out at sales@monumetric.com. 

All the best,
Your Monumetric Onboarding Team

SmartZones
Monumetric SmartZones is a proprietary ad-serving technology that intelligently optimizes each of your ad spaces for maximum revenue. Having this tech on your site ensures you don’t miss out on premium CPM advertising campaigns that may require specific or custom ad sizes that may not have been configured in a traditional ad zone.
SmartZones create higher bid competition from advertisers, which drives up CPMs and enables our pricing engines to sell your ads for the highest possible prices, all without taking up any additional space on your page or shifting content.


Here is a breakdown of what your strategy could look like, by ad type:

  *   Leaderboard: High-performing unit designated to be above content. 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320 X100 (mobile).
  *   Flight: In-content dynamically placed ads: Our Flight technology will scan the page and create custom ad placement based on word count or pixel count threshold. It does not interfere with user experience flow and will populate based on your preset parameters. This will intelligently generate ad units for both desktop and mobile users. 728x90, 300X250, and 320X50 (desktop), 300X250 and 320X50 (tablet), 320x100, 320X50 and 320X50 (mobile).
  *   Sidebar (flex ads)Desktop: These ads will fire in 1 of 3 non-intrusive sizes depending on the device (300X250, 300X600, and 160X600). Sizing depends on the device and which size gets the highest bid.
  *   In-screen: This one is a big one for you. This 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320X100 (Mobile) unit floats at the bottom of the screen. It can be closed by selecting "Close" in its top right corner.
  *   Pillar: A 160x600 ad (may flex to a 300x250 or 300x600 upon request) that takes advantage of the underutilized corners of the screen to display a highly viewable ad. Pillar ads only run on desktop, resulting in improved CPMs but fewer impressions due to it not running on every generated pageview.
  *   Interstitial - mobile, tablet, and desktop: Interstitial ads are high-performing, full-screen ads that cover the interface of your site. They're typically displayed at natural transition points in the flow of an app, such as between activities or during the pause between levels in a game. Because of the high attention required for this ad, it is set at a frequency of 1 ad per visitor per hour. Once you opt-in, you will be in control of increasing revenue for your site by toggling this ad on (and off) at your convenience.
  *   VOLT VIDEO: We offer a premium video product called VOLT, which supports pre-roll and mid-roll video ads. Our video ads perform anywhere from 2-6x what a typical display ad would earn in that same placement. The best part about VOLT (besides the revenue potential) is that we provide all of the highly engaging content that matches your niche for you. We have a mix of niche-specific content and top-tier editorial content to ensure your audience sees as relevant video content as possible.

Ads are ready to be implemented or go live?

I just want to be sure I’m aligned on expectations and not missing anything on my end.
Thank you,
Cade

Hi Cade,

Thanks so much for sharing those details about your site! It really helps us understand what makes your site and setup unique and how we can best support you. To answer your question, the onboarding fee needs to be paid upfront and can't be applied to future earnings.

At this point, your URL is in our advertiser review process, which normally takes a week or two to complete. This means our team will be working with our premium advertising partners to get the best ad budgets aligned to buy on your URL. Throughout the process, we will provide occasional updates via your onboarding dashboard, which can be reached at onboarding.monumetric.com<http://onboarding.monumetric.com/>.

You will be sent an email by Google to the listed GAM email address, and you will need to grant Monumetric permission to manage your inventory in Google Ad Manager by accepting the MCM invitation. Please keep an eye out for that email.

In order to receive full advertiser approvals, you will need to update/add the provided ads.txt file to your root directory so that when you visit "pennycentral.com/ads.txt<http://pennycentral.com/ads.txt>" you will see the raw txt file displayed.

The ads.txt file can easily be edited and updated with proper access to the root domain.

Here’s how it works for WordPress users:

  *   Install ads.txt plugin on your WordPress-managed website.
  *   On the left panel, click on Settings > Ads.txt to customize the settings.
  *   Place your Ads.txt details and Save the settings.

If you don’t want to use a plugin:

  *   From the left navigation panel of WordPress Dashboard, click Media.
  *   Click on the Add New button.
  *   Upload the ads.txt file.
  *   Wait for search engine bots to crawl it.

The ads.txt lines that need to be added can be accessed by clicking on the following link: https://monu.delivery/adstxt/common/ads.txt


Please let me know once this has been completed or if you have any issues or questions.
_________________________________________________________________________
Once I feel like we have enough advertisers for your site, here's an outline of the steps it will take to get your site live and optimized

Mock-Up Ad Strategy:

  *   I'll send you an Ad Strategy template configured by our yield and ad/ops teams to earn the most revenue on your site's desktop and mobile versions without harming the user experience. We'll review our SmartZone offering<https://www.monumetric.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Ads-Catalog-2022.pdf> in more detail, and we'll collaborate on the Ad Strategy to ensure it's exactly what you want before moving forward.

Implementation:

  *   Once we've agreed on the ad strategy, we'll need temporary admin credentials for your site to install the ads, and you will be prompted to make the $99 implementation payment. You will be prompted to input these credentials in your onboarding dashboard at onboarding.monumetric.com<http://onboarding.monumetric.com/>.

Going Live:

  *   Once your ads are installed properly, your Publisher Console will be activated, and we'll wait to see how the first day or two's worth of data looks to ensure we're on the right path to meet your revenue goals. If any changes need to be made, we'll make sure to fine-tune everything so you feel confident in our advertiser's performance.
  *   Ad performance will ramp up over 30 days as advertisers start to align campaign dollars with your URL. If you add video ads, they will also have their own 30-day ramp-up performance period.
  *   I'll then introduce you to your Publisher Support Team, who will continue to be a resource for you moving forward if you need to make changes to your ads, fix any issues (yes, the ad industry is imperfect, and there will be issues), or have any questions.

Publisher Console:

  *   Within the Monumetric Console, you will find informative data about how your ads are performing. While this data is useful to most publishers, we understand that sometimes the metrics posted may not make much sense initially. Our ad optimization team is fully equipped to help meet your data needs.

Getting Paid:

  *   Monumetric pays publishers monthly on net 60 terms on or before the 15th day of each month. We view our relationship as a partnership, and we work with you every step of the way to ensure you are satisfied with the ad strategy and revenue.

Things to Note:

  *   If you already have ads on your site, most publishers will not remove them until they're approved to work with Monumetric.

There is a ramp-up period of typically 30 days when you join our network. During this time, advertisers are still acclimating to your site and determining their spending.

HelloCade,

I have good news! I heard back from our Sales Lead, who has approved that we can move forward with your site, and I have moved it to the Ascend Program. :) Your site has also been approved by our ad provider,s and I'd like to review your ad strategy with you.

Below, I've included for you a comprehensive outline of the ad strategy we will be implementing on your site. When creating this strategy, we take into account many criteria, such as mobile vs. desktop users, time on page, site layout, browsers used, and much more.

SmartZones
Monumetric SmartZones is a proprietary ad-serving technology that intelligently optimizes each of your ad spaces for maximum revenue. Having this tech on your site ensures you don’t miss out on premium CPM advertising campaigns that may require specific or custom ad sizes that may not have been configured in a traditional ad zone.
SmartZones create higher bid competition from advertisers, which drives up CPMs and enables our pricing engines to sell your ads for the highest possible prices, all without taking up any additional space on your page or shifting content.


Here is a breakdown of what your strategy could look like, by ad type:

Leaderboard: High-performing unit designated to be above content. 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320 X100 (mobile).
Flight: In-content dynamically placed ads: Our Flight technology will scan the page and create custom ad placement based on word count or pixel count threshold. It does not interfere with user experience flow and will populate based on your preset parameters. This will intelligently generate ad units for both desktop and mobile users. 728x90, 300X250, and 320X50 (desktop), 300X250 and 320X50 (tablet), 320x100, 320X50 and 320X50 (mobile).
Sidebar (flex ads)Desktop: These ads will fire in 1 of 3 non-intrusive sizes depending on the device (300X250, 300X600, and 160X600). Sizing depends on the device and which size gets the highest bid.
In-screen: This one is a big one for you. This 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320X100 (Mobile) unit floats at the bottom of the screen. It can be closed by selecting "Close" in its top right corner.
Pillar: A 160x600 ad (may flex to a 300x250 or 300x600 upon request) that takes advantage of the underutilized corners of the screen to display a highly viewable ad. Pillar ads only run on desktop, resulting in improved CPMs but fewer impressions due to it not running on every generated pageview.
Interstitial - mobile, tablet, and desktop: Interstitial ads are high-performing, full-screen ads that cover the interface of your site. They're typically displayed at natural transition points in the flow of an app, such as between activities or during the pause between levels in a game. Because of the high attention required for this ad, it is set at a frequency of 1 ad per visitor per hour. Once you opt-in, you will be in control of increasing revenue for your site by toggling this ad on (and off) at your convenience.
VOLT VIDEO: We offer a premium video product called VOLT, which supports pre-roll and mid-roll video ads. Our video ads perform anywhere from 2-6x what a typical display ad would earn in that same placement. The best part about VOLT (besides the revenue potential) is that we provide all of the highly engaging content that matches your niche for you. We have a mix of niche-specific content and top-tier editorial content to ensure your audience sees as relevant video content as possible.


Please let me know if you have any preferences we should consider. I will submit this strategy to our team to begin configuring the ad code we'll use for your site's ad implementation.


In case you need it, here is a link to our ads catalog, which details Smartzones and our available ad sizes and units. Keep an eye on your console; you will be able to see what we are doing here on our end and if we need anything from you.

Please let me know if you have any questions!

Hi Samantha,
Thanks for your note. I want to address the tier placement and traffic concerns so we can move forward on the right foot.
On the Traffic Legitimacy Question:
You asked if I'm purchasing traffic because most of my visitors are from the US. I'm not—and I'm happy to provide proof.
PennyCentral serves the Home Depot penny deal community. Home Depot is a US retailer, and the penny deals phenomenon is almost exclusively American. I'm an administrator of the "Home Depot One Cent Items" Facebook group (https://www.facebook.com/groups/homedepotonecent), which has 63,000 members. The website is an extension of that community, which is why 97.6% of my traffic is US-based. Screenshot of the Facebook group is included (see attached).
My traffic sources confirm this is organic:

57% Direct (bookmarks/typed URL—can't be faked)
22% Organic Social (referrals from the Facebook group)
13% Organic Search (Google sending users for queries like "penny central" and "home depot penny items")
1% Referral (other sites linking to me)

Engagement metrics further disprove purchased/bot traffic:

Bounce rate: 18-28% on core pages (purchased traffic is typically 80-95%)
Avg engagement time: 1-2+ minutes (bots don't linger)
Pages per session: 1.54
Scroll events: 53% of users actively scrolling

I'm happy to provide:

Read-only Google Analytics access
Proof of Facebook group admin status
Additional engagement or retention metrics

If there's a specific data point you need to verify legitimacy, let me know and I'll get it to you.
On the Tier Placement:
I want to make sure I'm being evaluated against the criteria Monumetric publishes. Here's where I'm confused:
Your join page (monumetric.com/join), application form, and every third-party publisher review I've read say the qualifying metric is monthly pageviews:

Propel: 10K-80K pageviews
Ascend: 80K-500K pageviews

My current traffic is 85,000 pageviews in the most recent 28-day period (Jan 14 - Feb 10). That puts me in the Ascend range.
In your Feb 4 email, you mentioned you "look at the session pageviews." In your Feb 11 email, you said you "look at the active users." These are different metrics, and neither one matches what's published on your website or in your Terms & Conditions.
Regarding the consistency requirement:
I understand wanting to see sustained traffic before tier placement. However,  Section 12.9 of your Terms & Conditions specifies that the two-consecutive-month requirement applies to "Propel sites" qualifying for "graduation" into Ascend.
I'm not a Propel site yet—I haven't paid the $99 setup fee, haven't gone live, and haven't served a single ad impression. I'm still in the application phase. My traffic grew from ~75K to 85K pageviews during the approval process.
Your published application criteria don't mention a historical consistency requirement for new applicants—just "Monthly Pageviews (According to Google Analytics)" with tier ranges.
So here's what I'm asking:
Can you clarify the actual qualifying metric for Ascend? If it's pageviews (as published), I'm at 85K and would qualify. If there's a different metric or a consistency requirement for new applicants that isn't documented on your join page, I'd appreciate seeing that in writing so I know exactly what I'm working toward.
I'm not trying to cause friction here—I just want to be evaluated against the criteria you advertise, and I want to make sure we're both working from the same rulebook.
Let me know what you need from me to move this forward.
Thanks, Cade

I've reviewed the full ad strategy and I'm ready to move forward with everything as proposed. Attached is a screenshot of my current GA4 dashboard for reference.
All ad units approved:

Leaderboard
Flight (in-content)
Sidebar flex ads
In-screen (sticky bottom)

VOLT Video

Tier placement - Ascend qualification: Before your team begins configuration, I want to confirm my program tier. As you can see in the attached screenshot, my last 30 days show 85K views, 25K sessions, and 17K active users - all up significantly from the previous period (views up 99.3%, sessions up 72.6%, users up 42.7%). That puts me well above the 80k pageview threshold for Ascend. I was originally placed in Propel, but my traffic has grown past that tier. Can you confirm I'll be moved to Ascend with its customized ad strategy, ad ops support, and updated revenue terms?
Technical context for your dev team (not WordPress): My site is a custom-built Next.js application (React/server-rendered). Key things your team will need to know:

Framework: Next.js (App Router) deployed on Vercel
Routing: Client-side navigation between pages (single-page app behavior). Ad scripts will need to handle route changes without full page reloads.
Page templates your script will encounter:

/ (homepage) - moderate content length
/penny-list - highest traffic page, dynamic/live-updating list content
/guide/* - long-form article pages (best candidates for Flight + VOLT)
/sku/* - hundreds of individual product pages, short-to-medium content
/store-finder - interactive map page

Mobile-dominant: ~80% of traffic is mobile

Pages to EXCLUDE from all ads:

/report-find (community submission form)
/login and any /auth routes
/privacy-policy, /terms-of-service, /support
/api/* routes

Page-specific preferences:

/penny-list: Highest traffic, mostly quick mobile check-ins (~80 seconds). Would like to discuss Flight ad spacing here since it's a dynamic list, not a traditional article.
/guide/* chapters: Long-form, high engagement (18-21% bounce rate). Ideal for Flight, interstitial, and VOLT.
/guide hub (landing page): Standard ads, but no VOLT.
/sku/* pages: Standard ads. These have very low bounce rates (2-7%) and there are hundreds of them.

Questions for your team:

How does your script handle client-side navigation in a single-page app? Will it need a hook for route changes, or does it detect them automatically?
Does VOLT autoplay? If so, is it muted by default?

Leaderboard: High-performing unit designated to be above content. 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320 X100 (mobile).

Pillar: A 160x600 ad (may flex to a 300x250 or 300x600 upon request) that takes advantage of the underutilized corners of the screen to display a highly viewable ad. Pillar ads only run on desktop, resulting in improved CPMs but fewer impressions due to it not running on every generated pageview.
Interstitial - mobile, tablet, and desktop: Interstitial ads are high-performing, full-screen ads that cover the interface of your site. VOLT VIDEO: We offer a premium video product called VOLT, which supports pre-roll and mid-roll video ads. Hi Cade,

Thanks so much for sharing those details about your site! It really helps us understand what makes your site and setup unique and how we can best support you. To answer your question, the onboarding fee needs to be paid upfront and can't be applied to future earnings.

At this point, your URL is in our advertiser review process, which normally takes a week or two to complete. This means our team will be working with our premium advertising partners to get the best ad budgets aligned to buy on your URL. Throughout the process, we will provide occasional updates via your onboarding dashboard, which can be reached at onboarding.monumetric.com.

You will be sent an email by Google to the listed GAM email address, and you will need to grant Monumetric permission to manage your inventory in Google Ad Manager by accepting the MCM invitation. Please keep an eye out for that email.

In order to receive full advertiser approvals, you will need to update/add the provided ads.txt file to your root directory so that when you visit "pennycentral.com/ads.txt" you will see the raw txt file displayed.

The ads.txt file can easily be edited and updated with proper access to the root domain.

Here’s how it works for WordPress users:

Install ads.txt plugin on your WordPress-managed website.
On the left panel, click on Settings > Ads.txt to customize the settings.
Place your Ads.txt details and Save the settings.

If you don’t want to use a plugin:

>From the left navigation panel of WordPress Dashboard, click Media.
Click on the Add New button.
Upload the ads.txt file.
Wait for search engine bots to crawl it.

The ads.txt lines that need to be added can be accessed by clicking on the following link: https://monu.delivery/adstxt/common/ads.txt

Please let me know once this has been completed or if you have any issues or questions.
_________________________________________________________________________
Once I feel like we have enough advertisers for your site, here's an outline of the steps it will take to get your site live and optimized

Mock-Up Ad Strategy:

I'll send you an Ad Strategy template configured by our yield and ad/ops teams to earn the most revenue on your site's desktop and mobile versions without harming the user experience. We'll review our SmartZone offering in more detail, and we'll collaborate on the Ad Strategy to ensure it's exactly what you want before moving forward.

Implementation:

Once we've agreed on the ad strategy, we'll need temporary admin credentials for your site to install the ads, and you will be prompted to make the $99 implementation payment. You will be prompted to input these credentials in your onboarding dashboard at onboarding.monumetric.com.

Going Live:

Once your ads are installed properly, your Publisher Console will be activated, and we'll wait to see how the first day or two's worth of data looks to ensure we're on the right path to meet your revenue goals. If any changes need to be made, we'll make sure to fine-tune everything so you feel confident in our advertiser's performance.
Ad performance will ramp up over 30 days as advertisers start to align campaign dollars with your URL. If you add video ads, they will also have their own 30-day ramp-up performance period.
I'll then introduce you to your Publisher Support Team, who will continue to be a resource for you moving forward if you need to make changes to your ads, fix any issues (yes, the ad industry is imperfect, and there will be issues), or have any questions.

Publisher Console:

Within the Monumetric Console, you will find informative data about how your ads are performing. While this data is useful to most publishers, we understand that sometimes the metrics posted may not make much sense initially. Our ad optimization team is fully equipped to help meet your data needs.

Getting Paid:

Monumetric pays publishers monthly on net 60 terms on or before the 15th day of each month. We view our relationship as a partnership, and we work with you every step of the way to ensure you are satisfied with the ad strategy and revenue.

Things to Note:

If you already have ads on your site, most publishers will not remove them until they're approved to work with Monumetric.

There is a ramp-up period of typically 30 days when you join our network. During this time, advertisers are still acclimating to your site and determining their spending.

While you're waiting, here is some light reading if you'd like to learn a little more about the ad industry!

https://www.monumetric.com/video/

Hi Samantha,
Thanks for your note. I want to address the tier placement and traffic concerns so we can move forward on the right foot.
On the Traffic Legitimacy Question:
You asked if I'm purchasing traffic because most of my visitors are from the US. I'm not—and I'm happy to provide proof.
PennyCentral serves the Home Depot penny deal community. Home Depot is a US retailer, and the penny deals phenomenon is almost exclusively American. I'm an administrator of the "Home Depot One Cent Items" Facebook group (https://www.facebook.com/groups/homedepotonecent), which has 63,000 members. The website is an extension of that community, which is why 97.6% of my traffic is US-based. Screenshot of the Facebook group is included (see attached).
My traffic sources confirm this is organic:

  *   57% Direct (bookmarks/typed URL—can't be faked)
  *   22% Organic Social (referrals from the Facebook group)
  *   13% Organic Search (Google sending users for queries like "penny central" and "home depot penny items")

HelloCade,

I have good news: your site has been approved by our ad providers. :) I'd like to review your ad strategy with you.

Below, I've included for you a comprehensive outline of the ad strategy we will be implementing on your site. When creating this strategy, we take into account many criteria, such as mobile vs. desktop users, time on page, site layout, browsers used, and much more.

SmartZones
Monumetric SmartZones is a proprietary ad-serving technology that intelligently optimizes each of your ad spaces for maximum revenue. Having this tech on your site ensures you don’t miss out on premium CPM advertising campaigns that may require specific or custom ad sizes that may not have been configured in a traditional ad zone.
SmartZones create higher bid competition from advertisers, which drives up CPMs and enables our pricing engines to sell your ads for the highest possible prices, all without taking up any additional space on your page or shifting content.


Here is a breakdown of what your strategy could look like, by ad type:

  *   Leaderboard: High-performing unit designated to be above content. 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320 X100 (mobile).
  *   Flight: In-content dynamically placed ads: Our Flight technology will scan the page and create custom ad placement based on word count or pixel count threshold. It does not interfere with user experience flow and will populate based on your preset parameters. This will intelligently generate ad units for both desktop and mobile users. 728x90, 300X250, and 320X50 (desktop), 300X250 and 320X50 (tablet), 320x100, 320X50 and 320X50 (mobile).
  *   Sidebar (flex ads)Desktop: These ads will fire in 1 of 3 non-intrusive sizes depending on the device (300X250, 300X600, and 160X600). Sizing depends on the device and which size gets the highest bid.
  *   In-screen: This one is a big one for you. This 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320X100 (Mobile) unit floats at the bottom of the screen. It can be closed by selecting "Close" in its top right corner.
  *   Pillar: A 160x600 ad (may flex to a 300x250 or 300x600 upon request) that takes advantage of the underutilized corners of the screen to display a highly viewable ad. Pillar ads only run on desktop, resulting in improved CPMs but fewer impressions due to it not running on every generated pageview.
  *   Interstitial - mobile, tablet, and desktop: Interstitial ads are high-performing, full-screen ads that cover the interface of your site. They're typically displayed at natural transition points in the flow of an app, such as between activities or during the pause between levels in a game. Because of the high attention required for this ad, it is set at a frequency of 1 ad per visitor per hour. Once you opt-in, you will be in control of increasing revenue for your site by toggling this ad on (and off) at your convenience.
  *   VOLT VIDEO: We offer a premium video product called VOLT, which supports pre-roll and mid-roll video ads. Our video ads perform anywhere from 2-6x what a typical display ad would earn in that same placement. The best part about VOLT (besides the revenue potential) is that we provide all of the highly engaging content that matches your niche for you. We have a mix of niche-specific content and top-tier editorial content to ensure your audience sees as relevant video content as possible.

Please let me know if you have any preferences we should consider. I will submit this strategy to our team to begin configuring the ad code we'll use for your site's ad implementation.

Throughout the process, we will provide occasional updates via your onboarding dashboard, which can be reached at onboarding.monumetric.com<http://onboarding.monumetric.com/>.

You will be sent an email by Google to the listed GAM email address, and you will need to grant Monumetric permission to manage your inventory in Google Ad Manager by accepting the MCM invitation. Please keep an eye out for that email.

In order to receive full advertiser approvals, you will need to update/add the provided ads.txt file to your root directory so that when you visit "pennycentral.com/ads.txt<http://pennycentral.com/ads.txt>" you will see the raw txt file displayed.

The ads.txt file can easily be edited and updated with proper access to the root domain.

Here’s how it works for WordPress users:

  *   Install ads.txt plugin on your WordPress-managed website.
  *   On the left panel, click on Settings > Ads.txt to customize the settings.
  *   Place your Ads.txt details and Save the settings.

If you don’t want to use a plugin:

  *   From the left navigation panel of WordPress Dashboard, click Media.
  *   Click on the Add New button.
  *   Upload the ads.txt file.
  *   Wait for search engine bots to crawl it.

The ads.txt lines that need to be added can be accessed by clicking on the following link: https://monu.delivery/adstxt/common/ads.txt


Please let me know once this has been completed or if you have any issues or questions.
_________________________________________________________________________
Once I feel like we have enough advertisers for your site, here's an outline of the steps it will take to get your site live and optimized

Mock-Up Ad Strategy:

  *   I'll send you an Ad Strategy template configured by our yield and ad/ops teams to earn the most revenue on your site's desktop and mobile versions without harming the user experience. We'll review our SmartZone offering<https://www.monumetric.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Ads-Catalog-2022.pdf> in more detail, and we'll collaborate on the Ad Strategy to ensure it's exactly what you want before moving forward.

Implementation:

  *   Once we've agreed on the ad strategy, we'll need temporary admin credentials for your site to install the ads, and you will be prompted to make the $99 implementation payment. You will be prompted to input these credentials in your onboarding dashboard at onboarding.monumetric.com<http://onboarding.monumetric.com/>.

Going Live:

  *   Once your ads are installed properly, your Publisher Console will be activated, and we'll wait to see how the first day or two's worth of data looks to ensure we're on the right path to meet your revenue goals. If any changes need to be made, we'll make sure to fine-tune everything so you feel confident in our advertiser's performance.
  *   Ad performance will ramp up over 30 days as advertisers start to align campaign dollars with your URL. If you add video ads, they will also have their own 30-day ramp-up performance period.
  *   I'll then introduce you to your Publisher Support Team, who will continue to be a resource for you moving forward if you need to make changes to your ads, fix any issues (yes, the ad industry is imperfect, and there will be issues), or have any questions.

Publisher Console:

  *   Within the Monumetric Console, you will find informative data about how your ads are performing. While this data is useful to most publishers, we understand that sometimes the metrics posted may not make much sense initially. Our ad optimization team is fully equipped to help meet your data needs.

Getting Paid:

  *   Monumetric pays publishers monthly on net 60 terms on or before the 15th day of each month. We view our relationship as a partnership, and we work with you every step of the way to ensure you are satisfied with the ad strategy and revenue.

Things to Note:

  *   If you already have ads on your site, most publishers will not remove them until they're approved to work with Monumetric.

There is a ramp-up period of typically 30 days when you join our network. During this time, advertisers are still acclimating to your site and determining their spending.

While you're waiting, here is some light reading if you'd like to learn a little more about the ad industry!
https://www.monumetric.com/website-monetization/
https://blog.monumetric.com/why-use-network-header-bidding-dcad6129e4e5
https://www.monumetric.com/about-us/
https://www.monumetric.com/video/

Let me know if you have questions about any of the above; thanks!

Hi there,

Thank you for following up. I’m happy to provide the details you need to move PennyCentral into review.

Site Ownership & Background

1. Are you the sole owner of the site? Is there anyone else we should bring into the conversation?

Yes, I’m the sole owner and operator. No additional owners or partners to include.

2. What is the primary focus of your site?

PennyCentral (www.pennycentral.com<http://www.pennycentral.com>) is focused on extreme clearance “penny” deals, primarily Home Depot penny items. It aggregates item info and community-submitted finds to help users see what items are being found and where.

3. When was the site created, and has its niche or content strategy changed over time?

The site launched on December 4, 2025. The niche has been consistent since launch. The main changes have been expanding content and features as the site grows, but the focus has not changed.

Monetization & Eligibility

4. Has your site ever had ad serving restricted by Google (AdSense or Ad Manager)?

No, to my knowledge it has not.

5. Do you currently have a Google Ad Manager account? If so, can you please confirm the email address associated with it?

Yes. The email associated with it is cadegallen@gmail.com<mailto:cadegallen@gmail.com>.

6. Who is your current ad provider, if any?

None currently. No programmatic ads have ever been live on the site.

7. What does your current average monthly programmatic revenue look like?

$0, since programmatic ads have never been live.

Propel setup payment

Understood on the one-time $99 Propel setup payment and what it covers. Quick question: is it possible to have this deducted from my first earnings instead of paying upfront?

Thanks again,
Cade

body:
All right, that works for me! Did you wanna have a phone call or email

On Tue, Feb 17, 2026 at 5:31 PM Monetization Specialists Monumetric <sales@monumetric.com<mailto:sales@monumetric.com>> wrote:
HelloCade,

I have good news! I heard back from our Sales Lead, who has approved that we can move forward with your site, and I have moved it to the Ascend Program. :) Your site has also been approved by our ad provider,s and I'd like to review your ad strategy with you.


Below, I've included for you a comprehensive outline of the ad strategy we will be implementing on your site. When creating this strategy, we take into account many criteria, such as mobile vs. desktop users, time on page, site layout, browsers used, and much more.

SmartZones
Monumetric SmartZones is a proprietary ad-serving technology that intelligently optimizes each of your ad spaces for maximum revenue. Having this tech on your site ensures you don’t miss out on premium CPM advertising campaigns that may require specific or custom ad sizes that may not have been configured in a traditional ad zone.
SmartZones create higher bid competition from advertisers, which drives up CPMs and enables our pricing engines to sell your ads for the highest possible prices, all without taking up any additional space on your page or shifting content.


Here is a breakdown of what your strategy could look like, by ad type:

  *   Leaderboard: High-performing unit designated to be above content. 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320 X100 (mobile).
  *   Flight: In-content dynamically placed ads: Our Flight technology will scan the page and create custom ad placement based on word count or pixel count threshold. It does not interfere with user experience flow and will populate based on your preset parameters. This will intelligently generate ad units for both desktop and mobile users. 728x90, 300X250, and 320X50 (desktop), 300X250 and 320X50 (tablet), 320x100, 320X50 and 320X50 (mobile).
  *   Sidebar (flex ads)Desktop: These ads will fire in 1 of 3 non-intrusive sizes depending on the device (300X250, 300X600, and 160X600). Sizing depends on the device and which size gets the highest bid.
  *   In-screen: This one is a big one for you. This 728x90 (desktop and tablet) and 320X50 or 320X100 (Mobile) unit floats at the bottom of the screen. It can be closed by selecting "Close" in its top right corner.
  *   Pillar: A 160x600 ad (may flex to a 300x250 or 300x600 upon request) that takes advantage of the underutilized corners of the screen to display a highly viewable ad. Pillar ads only run on desktop, resulting in improved CPMs but fewer impressions due to it not running on every generated pageview.
  *   Interstitial - mobile, tablet, and desktop: Interstitial ads are high-performing, full-screen ads that cover the interface of your site. They're typically displayed at natural transition points in the flow of an app, such as between activities or during the pause between levels in a game. Because of the high attention required for this ad, it is set at a frequency of 1 ad per visitor per hour. Once you opt-in, you will be in control of increasing revenue for your site by toggling this ad on (and off) at your convenience.
  *   VOLT VIDEO: We offer a premium video product called VOLT, which supports pre-roll and mid-roll video ads. Our video ads perform anywhere from 2-6x what a typical display ad would earn in that same placement. The best part about VOLT (besides the revenue potential) is that we provide all of the highly engaging content that matches your niche for you. We have a mix of niche-specific content and top-tier editorial content to ensure your audience sees as relevant video content as possible.

Please let me know if you have any preferences we should consider. I will submit this strategy to our team to begin configuring the ad code we'll use for your site's ad implementation.


In case you need it, here is a link to our ads catalog<https://www.monumetric.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Ads-Catalog-2022.pdf>, which details Smartzones and our available ad sizes and units. Keep an eye on your console; you will be able to see what we are doing here on our end and if we need anything from you.

Please let me know if you have any questions!

From: Cade allen [cadegallen@gmail.com<mailto:cadegallen@gmail.com>]
Sent: 2/12/2026, 4:36 PM
To: sales@monumetric.com<mailto:sales@monumetric.com>
Subject: Re: Thanks for applying!

Hi Samantha,
Thanks for your note. I want to address the tier placement and traffic concerns so we can move forward on the right foot.
On the Traffic Legitimacy Question:
You asked if I'm purchasing traffic because most of my visitors are from the US. I'm not—and I'm happy to provide proof.
PennyCentral serves the Home Depot penny deal community. Home Depot is a US retailer, and the penny deals phenomenon is almost exclusively American. I'm an administrator of the "Home Depot One Cent Items" Facebook group (https://www.facebook.com/groups/homedepotonecent), which has 63,000 members. The website is an extension of that community, which is why 97.6% of my traffic is US-based. Screenshot of the Facebook group is included (see attached).
My traffic sources confirm this is organic:

  *   57% Direct (bookmarks/typed URL—can't be faked)
  *   22% Organic Social (referrals from the Facebook group)
  *   13% Organic Search (Google sending users for queries like "penny central" and "home depot penny items")
  *   1% Referral (other sites linking to me)

Engagement metrics further disprove purchased/bot traffic:

  *   Bounce rate: 18-28% on core pages (purchased traffic is typically 80-95%)
  *   Avg engagement time: 1-2+ minutes (bots don't linger)
  *   Pages per session: 1.54
  *   Scroll events: 53% of users actively scrolling

I'm happy to provide:

  *   Read-only Google Analytics access
  *   Proof of Facebook group admin status
  *   Additional engagement or retention metrics

If there's a specific data point you need to verify legitimacy, let me know and I'll get it to you.
________________________________
On the Tier Placement:
I want to make sure I'm being evaluated against the criteria Monumetric publishes. Here's where I'm confused:
Your join page (monumetric.com/join<http://monumetric.com/join>), application form, and every third-party publisher review I've read say the qualifying metric is monthly pageviews:

  *   Propel: 10K-80K pageviews
  *   Ascend: 80K-500K pageviews

My current traffic is 85,000 pageviews in the most recent 28-day period (Jan 14 - Feb 10). That puts me in the Ascend range.
In your Feb 4 email, you mentioned you "look at the session pageviews." In your Feb 11 email, you said you "look at the active users." These are different metrics, and neither one matches what's published on your website or in your Terms & Conditions.
Regarding the consistency requirement:
I understand wanting to see sustained traffic before tier placement. However, Section 12.9 of your Terms & Conditions specifies that the two-consecutive-month requirement applies to "Propel sites" qualifying for "graduation" into Ascend.
I'm not a Propel site yet—I haven't paid the $99 setup fee, haven't gone live, and haven't served a single ad impression. I'm still in the application phase. My traffic grew from ~75K to 85K pageviews during the approval process.
Your published application criteria don't mention a historical consistency requirement for new applicants—just "Monthly Pageviews (According to Google Analytics)" with tier ranges.
So here's what I'm asking:
Can you clarify the actual qualifying metric for Ascend? If it's pageviews (as published), I'm at 85K and would qualify. If there's a different metric or a consistency requirement for new applicants that isn't documented on your join page, I'd appreciate seeing that in writing so I know exactly what I'm working toward.
I'm not trying to cause friction here—I just want to be evaluated against the criteria you advertise, and I want to make sure we're both working from the same rulebook.
Let me know what you need from me to move this forward.
Thanks, Cade


________________________________

Hi Cade,

Thanks for all of this information. As I mentioned before, we look at the active user,s which is around 17K, and wouldn't qualify for our Ascend Program, especially because this is even lower in November and December, and we want to see this consistently for at least 3 months. If you still want to move forward, you would need to pay $99.

Also, as I reviewed your site again to see if it could be considered for our Ascend Program, I noticed that almost all your traffic is US. Is there a reason for this? This seems like you could be purchasing traffic, which would be a concern.

Please let us know. We look forward to hearing from you.
